assertions.title: Cancel running query

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3100 and select neurosynth engine
Load CBMA Spatial Prior example query into the editor
steps.expected: Example query appears in code editor
steps.observed: Query loaded: 'VoxelReported (x, y, z, study) :- PeakReported...'
Query started running, 'Running...' button and 'Cancel running query' button appeared

steps.action: Take screenshot showing Cancel button visible
steps.observed: Cancel button visible in UI alongside 'Running...' button

steps.expected: Query stops and 'Query cancelled.' message is shown
steps.observed: WebSocket onerror fires after ws.close(), showing 'WebSocketError: A WebSocket error occurred. The backend may be unavailable.' instead of 'Query cancelled.' message

evidence.network: WebSocket ws://localhost:3100/v1/statementsocket - closed before connection established (cancel race condition)

assertions.issues: FAIL: Cancel button IS visible during running state (pass), but clicking Cancel shows 'WebSocketError' instead of 'Query cancelled.' The root cause: cancelQuery() calls ws.close() which triggers ws.onerror asynchronously. The onerror handler lacks a guard and overwrites executionStatus from 'cancelled' to 'error'. The ws.onclose handler HAS a guard (checks wsRef.current === ws), but ws.onerror does NOT. Fix: add a cancellation flag (e.g., isCancellingRef) and check it in ws.onerror to skip the error transition.

steps.expected: Engine selected and examples panel visible
steps.observed: Destrieux engine selected, Examples panel shown in sidebar with 'Union of atlas regions' and 'Sulcal identification'

steps.action: Check example queries panel is visible
steps.expected: Examples section with predefined queries visible
steps.observed: Examples panel rendered with 'Collapse examples panel' toggle and two example items

steps.action: Click 'Union of atlas regions' example title button
steps.expected: Example query loads into code editor
steps.observed: CodeMirror editor now contains: 'LeftRegion(s, r):- destrieux(s, r) & startswith("L", s)...' (full query loaded)

steps.action: Verify query is loaded from /v2/examples/destrieux API
steps.observed: Query matches API response for 'destrieux1' example (Union of Destrieux atlas regions)

steps.action: Select destrieux engine and type query that returns no results

steps.expected: 'No results' message shown instead of empty/broken table
steps.observed: 'No results found' message shown in results panel (.no-results-message element)

steps.action: Ensure destrieux engine selected, query in editor
steps.observed: Editor contains 'NoRegion(s) :- destrieux(s, r) & startswith("ZZZ", s)'

steps.action: Click on code editor to focus it

steps.expected: Query submits same as clicking Run Query
steps.observed: Query submitted via keyboard shortcut - WebSocket connection opened, query executed, 'No results found' shown
Confirmed in CodeEditor.tsx: keymap.of([{ key: 'Mod-Enter', run: () => { onSubmitRef.current?.() ... } }]) - CM6's 'Mod' maps to Cmd on Mac

evidence.network: WebSocket connection created and query submitted

frictions.description: Cancel button test required using MutationObserver JS injection because the query completes too quickly to manually click Cancel with agent-browser timing
frictions.resolution: Used MutationObserver to click cancel immediately when the button appeared in the DOM

frictions.description: The 'No results found' panel is rendered off-screen and requires JavaScript scrollIntoView to capture in a screenshot
frictions.resolution: Used JS eval with scrollIntoView to bring the results panel into viewport

frictions.description: CodeMirror 6 editor does not expose its EditorView via DOM properties; keyboard type and click methods used instead of direct CM API
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser click + keyboard type to set editor content

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-EXEC-012, VAL-EXEC-013, VAL-POLISH-004), 1 failed (VAL-EXEC-011: Cancel button is visible but clicking it shows 'WebSocketError' instead of 'Query cancelled.' due to a race condition where ws.onerror fires after ws.close() and overwrites the 'cancelled' execution status)
